notes.content: Consensus direction corrected on 2026-05-21: use TiKV raft-rs (`raft` crate 0.7.0) instead of OpenRaft. Current integration only validates bootstrap (`MemStorage + RawNode`) behind `scheduler-server::cluster::raft_rs`; raft mode must remain unknown/not-schedulable until real event loop, transport, persistence, and leader fencing are implemented.

Phase2 raft-rs follow-up: added no-FK `raft_log_entries` and `raft_snapshots` storage foundations plus repository helpers. Reserved `/api/v1/raft/append-entries` now uses a raft-rs-message-shaped DTO but remains non-mutating (`accepted=false`) until runtime event loop/Ready persistence/fencing are implemented.

Raft HTTP transport now includes DTO -> `raft::eraftpb::Message` conversion/validation (message and entry type allowlists, non-negative numeric checks, base64 decode). Endpoint remains non-mutating and returns `accepted=false` until raft-rs runtime inbox/event loop exists.

Raft mode with storage now starts `RaftRuntimeCoordinator`: 100ms RawNode tick loop, Ready persistence order HardState -> entries -> snapshot -> advance. It does not campaign, does not send outbound messages, and still keeps `can_schedule=false` plus null leader_fencing_token.

Phase2 raft-rs now has deterministic in-process 3-node RawNode harness coverage: real campaign/election persists leader fencing before can_schedule=true, and membership proposal ConfChange commits/applies to raft_membership_proposals, raft_metadata.conf_state, and raft_members. Production Ready handling also syncs HardState/log/snapshot/commit into MemStorage before advance_append.

Phase2 raft-rs restart hardening: runtime startup restores MemStorage HardState/log entries from raft_metadata/raft_log_entries and clears stale leader_fencing_token before any role observation, so scheduler authority must be regenerated by current real leader state after restart. HTTP/Docker bridge transport smoke remains next prompt 060.

Phase2 raft HTTP route smoke added: valid x-scheduler-raft-token bypasses human session auth and enqueues /api/v1/raft/append-entries into runtime inbox; wrong token returns unauthorized envelope. This does not grant leadership; Docker bridge multi-container E2E remains prompt 061.

Phase2 raft Docker bridge E2E closed: scripts/raft-bridge-e2e.sh builds alpine runtime image, starts 3 scheduler containers on Docker bridge (no host network), uses container DNS peers scheduler-N:9090, injects SCHEDULER__CLUSTER__TRANSPORT_TOKEN, checks health/cluster/diagnostics/append, wrong-token 401, and max one fenced schedulable leader.

Phase3 audit foundation extended: audit_logs now carries before/after, trace_id, result, failure_reason with no FK; route helper reads x-request-id/x-trace-id; API/Web expose new fields. Export governance intentionally split to prompt 063 for row limits/redaction/content-type decisions.

Phase3 audit export governance: added GET /api/v1/audit-logs:export?format=json with audit:read, same filters, 500-row cap, JSON envelope, governance metadata, and Web JSON download. CSV intentionally rejected until redaction/content-type policy is designed. Next prompt 064: dangerous-action confirmation + permission-aware Web actions.

Frontend permission-aware actions now use web/src/components/Permission.tsx: useCan, PermissionGate, GuardedButton. Apply to mutating/dangerous UI actions so RBAC hides unavailable actions and Popconfirm gates dangerous operations. Jobs/Users/Scripts/Workflows updated in phase 064.

2026-05-22 Phase064 complete: Web dangerous/mutating actions now use PermissionGate/GuardedButton or Popconfirm; Jobs/Users/Scripts/Workflows actions are hidden by RBAC and destructive/lifecycle/runtime mutations require confirmation. Full verification passed: cargo fmt --check, clippy -D warnings, cargo test, cargo run -- --help, web typecheck/build (Vite chunk-size warning only). Next: .prompt/065 route metadata, lazy loading, unified 401/403, URL query governance.

2026-05-22 Phase065 in progress/implemented: route metadata source web/src/routes.tsx, lazy loaded routed pages, shared ForbiddenPage/RouteFallback, API auth error handler (401 clears token/login, 403 forbidden page), URL query persistence for audit/jobs/scripts/workflows via useUrlQueryState. Targeted checks passed: web typecheck and bun test. Next prompt created: .prompt/066-phase3-wasm-sandbox-processor-spike.md.

2026-05-22 Phase066: WASM sandbox boundary lives in scheduler-core as a stable worker contract: WasmProcessorSpec { runtime: wasmtime, entrypoint, resources, capabilities }. Defaults: _start, 30s timeout, 64MiB memory, 100M fuel, no network, no preopened dirs, explicit env list. Current crates.io search shows wasmtime 45.0.0; docs support fuel/epoch/resource-limiter controls. Next: worker-side executor using Wasmtime 45.x.

2026-05-22 Phase067: added crates/scheduler-wasm as worker-only Wasmtime 45.0.0 executor boundary. WasmExecutor enforces WasmProcessorSpec via fuel metering, epoch interruption timeout hook, StoreLimits memory cap, no WASI imports/ambient host access. Tests cover WAT smoke, network rejection, missing entrypoint, fuel exhaustion. Server HTTP/storage do not depend on Wasmtime.

2026-05-22 Phase068: worker proto now carries DispatchTask.processor_binding -> WasmProcessorBinding. scheduler-server dispatcher uses ScriptRepository to attach WASM binding only for processor_name script:<id> when script is language=wasm, status=approved, and WasmProcessorSpec validates (default-deny network/fs). Server passes module/policy metadata only; it still never executes user code. Next: SDK execution adapters.

2026-05-22 Java SDK Gradle verification filled: sdks/java/gradlew now defaults to Gradle 9.5.1 and runs from sdks/java project root. Default distribution URL uses Huawei Cloud mirror with GRADLE_DISTRIBUTION_URL override. Verified: ./gradlew --version --no-daemon and ./gradlew test --no-daemon pass. Gradle warns deprecated features must be cleaned before Gradle 10.

directives.directive: Default all future implementation work to functional/module-level file splitting across Rust server/crates, SDKs in any language, and web code. Avoid large single files; when a file grows materially, split by responsibility before or during the change.
